title: Excalibur: Sword of Promised Victory
content:
Excalibur is the primary Noble Phantasm of Artoria Pendragon (Saber). Classified as an Anti-Fortress Noble Phantasm, it is a divine construct — not something made by humans but a crystallization of humanity's wishes and dreams, forged at the planet's core and later entrusted to Artoria by the fairy Viviane, the Lady of the Lake.

Excalibur converts Artoria's magical energy (prana) into light of the highest purity and discharges it as a blade of concentrated energy. The destructive force is phenomenal — in the Fate route, a fully-powered Excalibur against Berserker (Heracles) destroyed five of his twelve lives in a single stroke.

To activate Excalibur, Artoria must remove its scabbard Avalon and announce the True Name: 'Excalibur — Sword of Promised Victory.' The release generates a massive torrent of golden-white light.

The strength of the attack scales directly with the amount of prana invested. Without sufficient prana supply from her Master, Artoria cannot unleash Excalibur at full power. During the Fourth Holy Grail War, Kiritsugu's refusal to supply prana (due to using Contraction Keyword to limit her) significantly hampered her combat effectiveness.

Excalibur is ranked A++ as a Noble Phantasm, placing it at the absolute apex of anti-fortress Noble Phantasms. It is one of the few weapons that Gilgamesh acknowledges in his treasury. The scabbard Avalon, a separate Noble Phantasm, grants the wielder immortality while active — when Shirou holds Avalon implanted in his body, he can survive otherwise fatal wounds.

title: Unlimited Blade Works: Infinite Creation of Swords
content:
Unlimited Blade Works is the Reality Marble Noble Phantasm of EMIYA (Archer) and later Shirou Emiya. It is an innate Bounded Field that compresses and reproduces the entire world inside the user's psyche, projecting it over reality to overwrite the external world with their inner world.

The world of Unlimited Blade Works is an infinite wasteland of hills and gears beneath a burning twilight sky, filled with thousands upon thousands of swords driven into the earth. It contains every weapon that EMIYA/Shirou has ever seen and recorded using his magical analysis ability — 'Tracing.' Within this Reality Marble, he can instantly call upon any weapon he has catalogued.

The incantation for activating Unlimited Blade Works is:
'I am the bone of my sword. Steel is my body and fire is my blood. I have created over a thousand blades. Unknown to Death, Nor known to Life. Have withstood pain to create many weapons. Yet, those hands will never hold anything. So as I pray, Unlimited Blade Works.'

Within his Reality Marble, EMIYA can use swords he has never physically held simply by analysing them from sight. The reproduced Noble Phantasms are one rank lower than the originals but are functionally identical. By invoking Broken Phantasm — loading excess prana into a projected Noble Phantasm until it explodes — he can use them as devastating explosive ammunition.

The Reality Marble is considered the embodiment of Shirou's core ideal: 'A sword that protects everyone.' His body is a furnace, his soul a sword, and every experience and memory he has is fuel for weapon creation.

In the Unlimited Blade Works route, Archer attempts to kill Shirou to prevent him from suffering the same fate of becoming a Counter Guardian. When they clash within Unlimited Blade Works itself, Shirou overrides Archer's superior technique with sheer volume and resolve, eventually creating a sword that surpasses Archer's projected Caliburn.

title: Gate of Babylon: King's Treasure
content:
Gate of Babylon is the primary Noble Phantasm of Gilgamesh, the King of Heroes. It is a golden portal that accesses his treasury — an extradimensional vault containing the originals of every Noble Phantasm in existence, because all legends trace back to Gilgamesh's collection from the Age of Gods.

As humanity spread across the world and heroes arose in different lands and eras, they took pieces of Gilgamesh's legend and mythologized them. This is why every famous legendary weapon — Thor's Mjolnir, Poseidon's trident, countless others — has a prototype somewhere in the Gate of Babylon.

In combat, Gilgamesh opens multiple Gates of Babylon simultaneously and launches Noble Phantasms as projectiles at his target, using them as javelins or stakes. This is referred to as his 'rain of Noble Phantasms.' He typically does not bother retrieving them after use, as he has infinite copies.

The Gate of Babylon is effective against Reality Marbles like Unlimited Blade Works because Gilgamesh's treasury predates all Noble Phantasms. Against Archer's copies, Gilgamesh fires the originals at greater power. However, Archer's advantage within his own Reality Marble is production speed — he can produce weapons faster than Gilgamesh can fire them.

Gilgamesh's greatest weapon within the Gate of Babylon is Ea, the Sword of Rupture, which predates human civilization itself and cannot be reproduced by any projection technique. It is classified as an Anti-World Noble Phantasm, capable of destroying the world.

In Fate/Zero, Gilgamesh uses Gate of Babylon extensively against other Servants. He fires chains from it (Enkidu: Chains of Heaven) to restrain the divine beast Caren and holds Rider (Iskandar) in place during their final battle.
